description: Create a three-column content slide on expected changes in first-line and second-line risk management headcount, with two horizontal bar charts and supporting commentary plus a quote callout.

header.kicker: Second annual EY/EVI global insurance risk management survey

items.text: Beyond reporting lines, our results also show increased coordination across the three lines of defense, including extensive engagement with internal audit and the business.
items.sub: These are signs of maturity, though a case can be made that these numbers, large as they are, should be even higher.

items.text: We believe there is opportunity at many insurers to align risk management methodologies and taxonomies across second-line programs and with internal audit.
More than half of CROs (52%) say the number of full-time risk management employees in the first line will increase, with 41% saying there will be no change.
items.sub: CROs expect relatively modest staff increases in the first line compared to last year, when 52% said there would be no impact on hiring.

center.title: Figure 21: How do you expect the number of full-time risk management professionals across the first line to change over the next five years?

items.text: Second-line resources will grow even more, our results suggest: 65% of CROs expect increases (up from 52% last year), versus 27% (down from 32%) who say there will be no change and 8% who expect decreasing headcount (down from 16%).
Business growth is driving the need for more risk management resources, according to 45% of CROs, while 29% cite regulatory factors as the primary cause.
items.sub: It also helps explain the ongoing push for automation within the risk management function.

items.text: Figure 22: How do you expect the number of full-time risk management professionals in the second line to change over the next five years?
items.sub: Increase > 25% 10%; Increase 16%-25% 11%; Increase 11%-15% 11%; Increase 6%-10% 18%; Increase 1%-5% 15%; No impact 27%; Decrease 6%-10% 8%.

items.text: CRO on access to key skills
items.sub: “We can co-source to supplement technical skills.”

footer.source: Note: Numbers may not add to 100 due to rounding.
